subject: vite 5.4.21 -> 6.4.3 (esbuild 0.21.5 -> 0.25.12 transitively)
methodology_statement: POST observation collected under the same validated observer (41f8c2ba) and the same mutation precondition gate (c70545f8) as PRE; execution context held constant except for dependency versions.
interpretation: CJS unchanged (634), ESM +15, distinct packages +23. Consistent with a major-version bump of an ESM-native toolchain: the CommonJS half did not move. No indication of instrumentation drift — the observer binary and its capability block are identical across both windows.
SUPPORTED: the patched versions were ACTUALLY LOADED during a real build (observed, not declared)
the build completed successfully at the new version (exit 0)
the observed delta is attributable to the dependency change, not to instrumentation or environment drift
NOT_SUPPORTED: that the underlying vulnerabilities are no longer exploitable
PATCHED VERSION OBSERVED != VULNERABILITY EXPLOITATION DISPROVEN
no adversarial request has been made against the dev server's fs.deny handling; behavioural verification remains OUTSTANDING
next_task: GOV-007-VITE: exercise the vite dev-server code paths (server.fs.deny handling, optimized-deps .map traversal) under adversarial conditions to determine whether the patched runtime still exhibits the vulnerable behaviour. Configuration evidence + behavioural evidence together are substantially stronger than either alone.
